$comment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4 char16_t constraint witness: u'😀' (U+1F600, a raw astral character with no universal-character-name escape) is a supplementary-plane code point that needs a UTF-16 surrogate PAIR — but ONE char16_t holds ONE code unit. The CST→HIR lowering FAILS LOUD with H_WideCharValueUnrepresentable (an unsuppressable code) rather than silently truncating to a wrong 16-bit unit. u'…' is always U16 (format-independent), so the reject is target-independent. Red-on-disable: drop the U16 range check in decodeWideCharCodepoint and this compiles emitting a wrong unit. The load-bearing pin is the CODE + a rejected compile at the literal's position.
